title: Carex pedicellata
taxonomy: Carex pedicellata belongs to the family Cyperaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sedges. This classification places it within the order Poales, which includes various grass-like plants and sedges. More specifically, Carex pedicellata can be identified as a member of the genus Carex, a large and diverse group of sedges. The species is further classified as a type of perennial sedge, with its classification also being described as a type of monocot plant. By understanding its classification, we can better appreciate the unique characteristics and traits of Carex pedicellata.

intro: Carex pedicellata, also known as the beaked sedge, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Carex pedicellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the roots and rhizomes of Carex pedicellata are often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in poultices and salves.
